schedule.description: NL Central division alarm — 7 of 9 losses
Peralta trade clock — 13 days to June 1

stories.title: Brewers 9, Cubs 3 — Imanaga Tagged in Game 1, Cubs Fall to 29-19
stories.angle: Imanaga's worst start of the season: 8 runs, 9 hits in 4.1 innings. Bauers 3-run HR in the 5th. Yelich homered for the 2nd straight day. Swanson 2-run HR for Cubs. Cubs fall to 29-19, Brewers pull to 0.5 GB. Cubs have lost 7 of last 9.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Imanaga got hammered. 8 runs, 9 hits in 4.1 innings — the worst start of his season.

Bauers 3-run HR. Yelich homered. Swanson's 2-run shot wasn't enough. Cubs fall to 29-19, Brewers now 0.5 GB.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

Brewers 9, Cubs 3 — Imanaga Tagged in Game 1
**Key facts + sources:**
| Fact | Source | Confidence |
|------|--------|------------|

| Imanaga record drops to 4-4 | ESPN recap | HIGH |
| Worst start of Imanaga's season | ESPN, Chicago Sun-Times | HIGH |
| Imanaga had given up 8 ER total over previous 5 starts combined | ESPN/Sun-Times context | HIGH |
| Jake Bauers: HR, 4 RBI (3-run HR in 5th off video board) | ESPN recap | HIGH |
| Christian Yelich: HR, 2 RBI (2nd consecutive day with HR) | ESPN, Yelich search | HIGH |
| Dansby Swanson: 2-run HR for Cubs | ESPN recap, CBS Sports | HIGH |
| Shane Drohan (2-1): 4.1 IP, 4 H, W | ESPN recap | HIGH |
| Sproat was pulled in 5th (Drohan replaced him) | ESPN recap | HIGH |
| Cubs lost 7th time in 9 games | Chicago Sun-Times, CBS Sports recap context | HIGH |
| Milwaukee pulled within half a game of division lead | ESPN recap | HIGH |
| Cubs fall to 29-19 | Derived from search: "29-18 before game + 1 loss" | MEDIUM |
| Brewers improve to 27-18 | Derived from standings search ("26-18 before game + 1 win") | MEDIUM |
**Fact-check note on Cubs record:** Story history May 18 pipeline recorded Cubs at 29-17 after the Crosstown Classic walkoff. However, search results indicate Cubs were 29-18 before the May 18 Brewers game (same date as the Crosstown Game 3). This is consistent: Crosstown Game 3 (walkoff loss) put Cubs at 29-18, then Brewers loss put them at 29-19. The May 18 pipeline entry of "Cubs fall to 29-17" appears to track only through Game 3, not both games on the same date. Today: Cubs 29-19. ✓

stories.title: NL Central Division Alarm — Seven Losses in Nine Games
stories.angle: Cubs 29-19. Brewers 0.5 GB. Cardinals 1.0 GB. All five NL Central teams above .500. The Cubs went 2-4 against the Braves and White Sox and now face a surging Brewers team at home. The division lead is evaporating.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Seven losses in nine games. Brewers half a game back. Cardinals one game out.

The Cubs aren't sleepwalking to a division title. They're being chased — and the gap is closing fast.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

stories.title: Game 2 Preview — Ben Brown vs Misiorowski, Wrigley, 6:40 PM CT
stories.angle: Brown (1-1, 1.60 ERA) has allowed 0 ER in his last 12.2 IP. Misiorowski (3-2, 2.12 ERA) leads MLB with 80 Ks in 51 innings and has 0 ER in 3 straight starts. One of the best pitching matchups of the week. Cubs need the win.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Brown vs Misiorowski tonight at Wrigley. 6:40 PM CT.

Ben Brown: 1.60 ERA, 0 ER in his last 12.2 innings.
Jacob Misiorowski: 2.12 ERA, MLB-best 80 Ks in 51 innings, 0 ER in 3 straight.

Cubs need this one.

#Cubs #GoCubs #CubsBaseball

stories.title: Peralta Trade Clock — 13 Days to June 1 Decision
stories.angle: Imanaga's blowup makes this more urgent. The Cubs' rotation can't survive on Imanaga alone. Mets' June 1 decision point is 13 days away. Peralta (3.12 ERA), Counsell connection, pending free agency = most logical deal in baseball.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Last night confirmed it: the Cubs' rotation can't survive on Imanaga alone.

Peralta's June 1 decision point is 13 days out. His 3.12 ERA, Counsell connection, and pending free agency make this the most logical deal in baseball.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

stories.title: Matt Boyd Bullpen Session Next Week — Late June Return on Track
stories.angle: Boyd threw off the mound May 16. Bullpen session expected this week. Late June return still on track. Positive news amid the rotation crisis — Boyd's return won't rescue the season but will help stabilize the back end.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Good news from the recovery front: Matt Boyd is throwing a bullpen session next week.

Late June return still on track. A healthy Boyd won't save the season — but he'll make it a lot more manageable.

#Cubs #GoCubs #CubsBaseball
